Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

dirdata controls LFSCK "-namespace" behavior. Your patch broken that. Please fix it. Thanks.

Patch Set 4: (1 inline comment)

Fan Yong, could you please explain further on how this patch breaks LFSCK namespace scanning?  It looks to me like the DIRDATA mount option is set in the superblock whenever the filesystem is mounted by Lustre.  The goal of this patch is to fix dirdata handling for non-Lustre mounts of the filesystem.

Otherwise, it isn't possible to access directories with dirdata enabled when mounting the filesystem with "-t ldiskfs" (e.g. for backup, or repair).

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+2

Fan Yong - the behaviour of Lustre with this patch should be the same as before. What is different is that when mounting as type ldiskfs it does not return the dirdata to userspace anymore.

This is done by requiring a mount option to return dirdata to the caller, but the osd-ldiskfs code supplies this mount option always, so lsutre should continue to work the same as before. Please take a closer look at the patch.

Patch Set 6:

Andreas: I mean that most of original case get_dtype() returned from the last branch with LDISKFS_DIRENT_LUFID, but now it returns from the branch above the last one without LDISKFS_DIRENT_LUFID.

Patch Set 6:

Fan Yong, the get_dtype() code is changed from returning LUFID when the INCOMPAT_DIRDATA feature is set in the disk superblock to returning LUFID when the DIRDATA mount option is set. The DIRDATA mount option is always set by osd-ldiskf, so it appears to be the same to me?

Patch Set 6:

According to current patch, "DIRDATA" is only enabled when "LDISKFS_MOUNT_OPT_DIRDATA" defined. But I did not found where "LDISKFS_MOUNT_OPT_DIRDATA" is defined. So with this patch, it will never return LUFID. Have I missed anything?

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch is broken and needs to be refreshed.

Patch Set 7:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/20607/

Patch Set 7: Verified-1

Build Failed 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/20607/ : FAILURE

Patch Set 7:

I have been thinking about the approach of this patch. Currently this patch does set_opt for the mount options after the mount has happened earlier in the function with vfs_kern_mount. I was thinking instead we just expand the options string to include also datadir besides no_mbcache. This also gives us the flexibility to mount ldiskfs with the dirdata option which we don't have with the current approach of this patch. This will require ldiskfs to support DIRDATA (Opt_dirdata) as a mount option in super.c.
Uploaded patch set 8.

parents.subject: LU-3467 ofd: remove obsoleted OBD methods

commit.subject: LU-2880 ldiskfs: Added mount option to enable dirdata.
commit.message:
LU-2880 ldiskfs: Added mount option to enable dirdata.

Added the code to set mount option for enabling the dirdata in
osd_mount(). This will set the dirdata option for lustre
filesystems only. The dirdata mount option would be checked in
get_dtype() to decide whether to pass dirdata flag as well while
reading the file type.

Signed-off-by: Manisha Salve <msalve@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I07f430c5cd7ad92b81746085b05b53c1202dd725
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6495
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
